response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong experience in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role specifically requires a C++ developer with deep STL, multi-threading, message-broker, and industrial automation expertise. C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (Dec 2014–Jun 2015), and there is no evidence of IEC 62443, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA), or Secure Development Lifecycle work. The mismatch on the core technical stack and domain is too significant to justify an interview for this specific position.
strengths: 12+ years of professional software engineering across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development, and event-driven architecture experience
Past C/C++ experience at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI plus low-latency async/fiber work in DLang at Dunnhumby
concerns: Only ~6 months of dedicated C++ work, over 10 years ago — no recent hands-on C++/STL evidence
No experience with IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No exposure to MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0), or automation machinery domain

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 background
Strong general software engineering and architecture experience
